result.stdout:
dotnet-serve 1.10.194+9d9dd6054a2bf57526cdf3a2e88309a56e28ecdc

A simple command-line HTTP server

Usage: dotnet serve [options]

Options:
  --version                            Show version information.
  -d|--directory <DIR>                 The root directory to serve. [Current
                                       directory]
  -o|--open-browser[:<PATH>]           Open a web browser when the server
                                       starts. [Default = false]
                                       You can also provide the subpath to
                                       launch by using -o:<PATH>.
                                       Example: -o:/path/index.html
  -p|--port <PORT>                     Port to use [8080]. Use 0 for a dynamic
                                       port.
  -a|--address <ADDRESS>               Address to use. [Default = localhost].
                                       Accepts IP addresses,
                                       'localhost' to accept only loopback
                                       requests, or
                                       'any' to accept requests from any IP
                                       address.
  --path-base <PATH>                   The base URL path appended to the site
                                       URL.
  --reverse-proxy <MAPPING>            Map a path pattern to another url.
                                       Expected format is
                                       <SOURCE_PATH_PATTERN>=<DESTINATION_URL_PREFIX>.
                                       SOURCE_PATH_PATTERN uses ASP.NET routing
                                       syntax. Use {**all} to match anything.
  --default-extensions[:<EXTENSIONS>]  A comma-delimited list of extensions to
                                       use when no extension is provided in the
                                       URL. [.html,.htm]
  -q|--quiet                           Show less console output.
  -v|--verbose                         Show more console output.
  -h|--headers <HEADER_AND_VALUE>      A header to return with all
                                       file/directory responses. e.g. -h
                                       "X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block"
  -S|--tls                             Enable TLS (HTTPS)
  --cert                               A PEM encoded certificate file to use for
                                       HTTPS connections.
                                       Defaults to file in current directory
                                       named 'cert.pem'
  --key                                A PEM encoded private key to use for
                                       HTTPS connections.
                                       Defaults to file in current directory
                                       named 'private.key'
  --pfx                                A PKCS#12 certificate file to use for
                                       HTTPS connections.
                                       Defaults to file in current directory
                                       named 'cert.pfx'
  --pfx-pwd                            The password to open the certificate
                                       file. (Optional)
  -m|--mime <MAPPING>                  Add a mapping from file extension to MIME
                                       type. Empty MIME removes a mapping.
                                       Expected format is <EXT>=<MIME>.
  -z|--gzip                            Enable gzip compression
  -b|--brotli                          Enable brotli compression
  --compression-level                  The level of compression to use, when
                                       compression is enabled
                                       Allowed values are: Optimal, Fastest,
                                       NoCompression, SmallestSize.
  -c|--cors                            Enable CORS (it will enable CORS for all
                                       origins and all methods)
  --save-options                       Save specified options to .netconfig for
                                       subsequent runs.
  --config-file                        Use the given .netconfig file.
  --fallback-file                      The path to a file which is served for
                                       requests that do not match known file
                                       names.
  -?|--help                            Show help information.

A simple command-line HTTP server

Usage: dotnet serve [options]

Options:
  --version                            Show version information.
  -d|--directory <DIR>                 The root directory to serve. [Current
                                       directory]
  -o|--open-browser[:<PATH>]           Open a web browser when the server
                                       starts. [Default = false]

                                       launch by using -o:<PATH>.
                                       Example: -o:/path/index.html
  -p|--port <PORT>                     Port to use [8080]. Use 0 for a dynamic
                                       port.
  -a|--address <ADDRESS>               Address to use. [Default = localhost].
                                       Accepts IP addresses,
                                       'localhost' to accept only loopback
                                       requests, or
                                       'any' to accept requests from any IP
                                       address.
  --path-base <PATH>                   The base URL path appended to the site
                                       URL.
  --reverse-proxy <MAPPING>            Map a path pattern to another url.
                                       Expected format is
                                       <SOURCE_PATH_PATTERN>=<DESTINATION_URL_PREFIX>.
                                       SOURCE_PATH_PATTERN uses ASP.NET routing
                                       syntax. Use {**all} to match anything.
  --default-extensions[:<EXTENSIONS>]  A comma-delimited list of extensions to
                                       use when no extension is provided in the
                                       URL. [.html,.htm]
  -q|--quiet                           Show less console output.
  -v|--verbose                         Show more console output.
  -h|--headers <HEADER_AND_VALUE>      A header to return with all
                                       file/directory responses. e.g. -h
                                       "X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block"
  -S|--tls                             Enable TLS (HTTPS)
  --cert                               A PEM encoded certificate file to use for
                                       HTTPS connections.
                                       Defaults to file in current directory
                                       named 'cert.pem'
  --key                                A PEM encoded private key to use for
                                       HTTPS connections.
                                       Defaults to file in current directory
                                       named 'private.key'
  --pfx                                A PKCS#12 certificate file to use for
                                       HTTPS connections.
                                       Defaults to file in current directory
                                       named 'cert.pfx'
  --pfx-pwd                            The password to open the certificate
                                       file. (Optional)
  -m|--mime <MAPPING>                  Add a mapping from file extension to MIME
                                       type. Empty MIME removes a mapping.
                                       Expected format is <EXT>=<MIME>.
  -z|--gzip                            Enable gzip compression
  -b|--brotli                          Enable brotli compression
  --compression-level                  The level of compression to use, when
                                       compression is enabled
                                       Allowed values are: Optimal, Fastest,
                                       NoCompression, SmallestSize.
  -c|--cors                            Enable CORS (it will enable CORS for all
                                       origins and all methods)
  --save-options                       Save specified options to .netconfig for
                                       subsequent runs.
  --config-file                        Use the given .netconfig file.
  --fallback-file                      The path to a file which is served for
                                       requests that do not match known file
                                       names.
  -?|--help                            Show help information.

options.description:
Address to use. [Default = localhost].
Accepts IP addresses,
'localhost' to accept only loopback requests, or
'any' to accept requests from any IP address.
Enable brotli compression

A PEM encoded certificate file to use for HTTPS connections.
Defaults to file in current directory named 'cert.pem'
The level of compression to use, when compression is enabled
Use the given .netconfig file.
Enable CORS (it will enable CORS for all origins and all methods)
A comma-delimited list of extensions to use when no extension is provided in the URL. [.html,.htm]
The root directory to serve. [Current directory]
A file to prevent from being served.
The path to a file which is served for requests that do not match known file names.
Enable gzip compression
A header to return with all file/directory responses. e.g. -h "X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block"

A PEM encoded private key to use for HTTPS connections.
Defaults to file in current directory named 'private.key'
For advanced diagnostics.

Add a mapping from file extension to MIME type. Empty MIME removes a mapping.
Expected format is <EXT>=<MIME>.

Open a web browser when the server starts. [Default = false]
You can also provide the subpath to launch by using -o:<PATH>.
Example: -o:/path/index.html
The base URL path appended to the site URL.

A PKCS#12 certificate file to use for HTTPS connections.
Defaults to file in current directory named 'cert.pfx'
The password to open the certificate file. (Optional)
Port to use [8080]. Use 0 for a dynamic port.
Show less console output.

Map a path pattern to another url.
Expected format is <SOURCE_PATH_PATTERN>=<DESTINATION_URL_PREFIX>.
SOURCE_PATH_PATTERN uses ASP.NET routing syntax. Use {**all} to match anything.
Save specified options to .netconfig for subsequent runs.
Enable TLS (HTTPS)
Show more console output.
